mnemonics.title: CI/CD Pipeline Stages
mnemonics.content: BUILD → TEST → STAGE → PRODUCTION - Remember: 'BTS' (Beautiful Test Stage) then 'P' (Production)

mnemonics.title: Deployment Strategies
mnemonics.content: BLUE-GREEN: Switch All | CANARY: Try Few | ROLLING: Step by Step | RECREATE: All at Once - Remember: BBCR (Blue Blue Can Roll)

acronyms.usage: Frequently merging code changes with automated builds and tests
Automating the delivery pipeline from code to production
Average time to recover from deployment failures
Using Git as single source for declarative infrastructure

visualTips.visualization: Think of two identical parallel roads (Blue and Green). Traffic switches from one to another instantly. Green becomes Blue when switch happens.
Imagine a canary in a coal mine - release to a small 'test' group first to detect danger before exposing everyone.
Imagine replacing wheels on a moving car one at a time - keep the car running while updating parts incrementally.
Think of an assembly line in a factory: code enters one end, gets tested and packaged, then emerges as a deployed product.

analogies.analogy: Like a conductor managing an orchestra - each musician (container) plays its part while the conductor (orchestrator) ensures timing and coordination.
Like renting a new car for each trip rather than maintaining one car - each journey starts with a fresh, reliable vehicle.
Like light switches in a house - you can turn features on/off without rewiring (redeploying code).
Like a water treatment plant - water (code) flows through multiple filter stages (tests) before reaching consumers (production).
